folio.label: 96r
folio.l: ¢ ñet wol I , q¶ this Markys softly
That in thy chaumbre , I and thow and she
Haue a colacione , and wost thow why
For I wol aske , if it hire wyl be
To be my wyf , and reule hire after me
And al this shal be don , in thy päsence
I wol nat speke , out of thyn audyence
¢ And in the Chaumbre , while they were a boute
Here tretee , whicú as ©e shuln after heere
The peeple cam , in to the hous witú oute
And wondred hem , in how honest manere
Ententifly , she kept hire fader dere
But vtterly , Grisildis wondir mygút
For neuäe erst , ne saw she , swicú a sigút
¢ No wonder is , thougú that she were astone¶
To se so gret a gest , come in to that place
She neuäe was , to swicú gestes woned
For whicú she loked , witú ful pale face
But shortly , fortú , this matere for to chace
Theise arn the wordes , that the Markys seyde
To this benygne , verray feithful Mayde
¢ Grisilde he seide , ©e shuln wel vnderstonde
It lyketú to ©oure fader , and to me
That I ©ow wedde , and eke it may so stonde
As I suppose , ©e wol that it so be
But this demaunde , aske I first q¶ he
That syn it shal be don , in hasty wyse
Wol ©e assent , or ellis ©ow a vyse
¢ I seye this , be ©e redy witú good hert
To al my lust , and that I freely may
As me best thynketú , do ©ow laugúe or smert
And neuäe ©e toto grucche it , nygút ne day
And eke whan I sey ©a , ©e sey nat nay
Neither be word , ne frounyngö countenaunce
Swere this , and heere I swere oure alliaunce
¢ Wondrynge vp on this thyngö , quakyngö for drede
She seyde , lord , vn digne and vnworthy
Am I , to thilke honour , that ©e me bede
But as ©e wol ©oure self , rigút so wol I
And heere I swere , that neuäe wyllyngly
In werk ne thougút , I nyl ©ow disobeye
For to be deed , thougú me were lotú to deye
¢ This is I now , Grisilde myn q¶ he
And fortú he gootú , witú a ful sobre cheer
Out at the dore , and after that cam she
And to the peeple he seide , in this manere
This is my wyf q¶ he , that stondetú heere
